subject: Physics
question_text: The magnetic flux through a coil is zero when the magnetic field is:
option_a: Perpendicular to the plane of the coil
option_b: Parallel to the plane of the coil
option_c: At 45 degrees to the plane of the coil
option_d: Equal to zero everywhere in space

subject: Physics
question_text: Faraday's second law of electromagnetic induction states that the induced EMF in a coil is directly proportional to:
option_a: The magnetic flux linked with the coil
option_b: The rate of change of magnetic flux linked with the coil
option_d: The area of the coil only
